steps.title: Recognize the arc-to-circle proportion
steps.content: The arc measure of 60° represents $\frac{60°}{360°} = \frac{1}{6}$ of the full circle. Therefore, the arc length must be the same fraction of the circumference. Setting up: $$4 = \frac{1}{6} \cdot C$$

steps.title: Solve for circumference
steps.content: Multiply both sides by 6: $$C = 4 \times 6 = 24 \text{ inches}$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
